iti88:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti88:2.1: “Monks, there are these three inside stains, inside enemies, inside foes, inside murderers, inside adversaries.
iti88:2.3: Greed is an inside stain, inside enemy, inside foe, inside murderer, inside adversary.
iti88:2.5: Delusion is an inside stain, inside enemy, inside foe, inside murderer, inside adversary.
iti88:2.6: These are the three inside stains, inside enemies, inside foes, inside murderers, inside adversaries.”
iti88:3.4: as a danger born from within.
iti88:4.1: A person, when greedy, doesn’t know his own welfare;
iti88:5.2: feels no greed for what would merit greed,
iti88:5.4: like a drop of water off a lotus leaf.
iti88:6.4: as a danger born from within.
iti88:7.1: A person, when aversive, doesn’t know his own welfare;
iti88:8.2: feels no aversion for what would merit aversion,
iti88:8.4: like a palm leaf from its stem.
iti88:9.4: as a danger born from within.
iti88:10.1: A person, when deluded, doesn’t know his own welfare;
iti88:11.2: feels no delusion for what would merit delusion,
iti88:11.4: as the rising of the sun, the dark.
